title: 10 best herbal essential oils for eye floaters
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for eye floaters are a natural and holistic approach to alleviate the distressing visual phenomenon of floaters. They help by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and promoting eye health. The best herbal essential oils for eye floaters include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Centella asiatica, Curcuma longa, Bacopa monnieri, Vitis vinifera, Ruscus aculeatus, Viscum album, Lavandula angustifolia, and Boswellia serrata. If left untreated, eye floaters can lead to anxiety, depression, and reduced quality of life, hindering daily activities and affecting overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its unique properties that promote relaxation, reduce stress, and soothe the eyes. The calming scent of lavender has been shown to ease eye tension and alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters, such as blurred vision and eye strain. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce irritation and discomfort caused by the tiny particles in the vitreous humor that create floaters. By incorporating English lavender essential oils into your daily routine, you can promote overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in lavender oil help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, potentially reducing the appearance of eye floaters.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the vitreous gel within the eyeball, which can alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters, such as blurred vision and sensitivity to light.

Anxiolytic (calming) properties: The calming effects of lavender oil can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common causes of eye strain that may exacerbate the appearance of eye floaters; by promoting relaxation, lavender oil may also alleviate associated symptoms like headaches and eye fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes related to eye floaters.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, particularly linoleic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for reducing eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dry, dark place for at least 24 hours or until they reach a moisture content of 12%.
Steam distill the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with deionized water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate and filter the essential oil from the distillation process using a glass container and paper filters, taking care not to contaminate the oil.
Bottle the pure lavandula angustifolia essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains a powerful compound called lutein, which is known to reduce inflammation in the eyes and improve vision clarity. Additionally, pot marigold essential oils contain flavonoids that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thus reducing the appearance of eye floaters. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, potentially alleviating symptoms of eye floaters.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold essential oil can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of eye floaters.

Vasodilatory: Pot marigold essential oil has been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms such as blurred vision and eye strain associated with eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and improve vision clarity.
Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant possess antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at dawn when dewy for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers using high-quality distillation equipment and filtered water at 100°c for 1 hour.
Collect and filter the essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles within an hour of distillation.
Store the calendula officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with eye floaters because its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the irritation and discomfort associated with these small, floating specks in one's vision. The oil's antioxidant properties also work to eliminate free radicals that can damage the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow. By applying curry plant essential oils topically around the eyes, individuals may experience relief from eye floaters and improved overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal curry plant essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like curcuminoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of herbal curry plant essential oil can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of eye floaters.

Stimulating property: Herbal curry plant essential oil has a stimulating effect on the brain and eyes, which can help improve blood circulation and reduce symptoms like blurred vision and eye strain associated with eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, curry plant roots help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to eye floaters.
Buds: The tender buds of the curry plant contain high levels of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum flowers in the early morning and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Combine 200g of the rinsed flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Transfer the flower-carrier oil mixture to a dark glass jar and store it at room temperature for 6 weeks.
Shake the jar daily for 20 minutes to facilitate the extraction process and monitor for any signs of spoilage.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, retaining the herbal essential oil for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with eye floaters because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated eyes. The oil's calming effects can reduce the appearance of eye floaters, which are small, harmless particles that can appear as spots or strings in one's vision due to aging or eye conditions. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal lemon balm essential oil can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters such as blurred vision and discomfort.
Sedative and calming properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil's sedative and calming effects can help soothe stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eye floaters and other eye problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to treat various eye disorders, including conjunctivitis and blepharitis, making them a suitable part for creating essential oils to address eye floaters.

Buds: The buds of the lemon balm plant are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which are known to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for reducing inflammation associated with eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in large quantities for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves with water at a ratio of 10:1 using a high-quality still for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter and store in dark glass bottles.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before using or sharing with others.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its remarkable calming properties. The oil has been traditionally used to soothe irritated eyes and reduce inflammation, which is often associated with eye floaters. When applied topically around the affected eye, chamomile oil can help relax the muscles and calm the nerves, reducing tension and discomfort caused by floating spots in the vision. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when their fragrance is most potent and potentate them immediately.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 20g of plant material in 100ml of water for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.
Transfer the filtered oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dry place for up to six months.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil at a ratio of 3-5% chamomilla essential oil and 95-97% carrier oil for topical use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can alleviate the discomfort associated with floaters. The oil's antioxidant-rich composition also helps to neutralize free radicals, preventing damage to the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. Inflammation-reducing properties: The oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the vitreous humor, a gel-like substance inside the eyeball where eye floaters often accumulate.

Antispasmodic and anti-tussive properties: St. John's Wort essential oil has been shown to relax muscles and soothe spasms, which can help alleviate eye strain and reduce symptoms associated with eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them effective for treating eye floaters.
Seeds: Contain triterpenoid compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the eyes from damage and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves in the morning.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves with 200 milliliters of water for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles.
Add 10 drops of the hypericum perforatum essential oil to 15 milliliters of carrier oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as echinacea, help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia flowers in the early morning for optimal oil content and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator set at 105°f for 12 hours or until crumbly texture is achieved.
Combine 100g of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers with 500ml of high-proof grain alcohol in a dark glass container for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard solids after 48 hours, allowing liquid to settle for 24 hours before separating oil layer.
Distill the resulting essential oil using a low-temperature steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2 hours to extract pure echinacea angustifolia oil with optimal therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats essential oils helps with eye floaters because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality avena sativa seeds in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the seeds in a warm, dry place for seven days or use a food dehydrator.
Blend one ounce of dried avena sativa seeds with four ounces of carrier oil and let it steep for ten hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container taking about twenty minutes.
Store the herbal essential oil in an amber glass bottle in a cool dark place for at least two weeks to mature fully.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves measure one cup of leaves.
Dry the plantago major leaves by spreading them on paper towels for two weeks.
Steam distill the dried plantago major leaves using a still and collect 20ml of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities take five minutes.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to a dark glass bottle store in a cool dry place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves from mature trees or purchase high-quality dried eucalyptus leaves in bulk.
Dry the eucalyptus leaves in a warm, dark place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content and enhance oil production.
Combine 100g of dried eucalyptus leaves with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for slow distillation of essential oils into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer the resulting eucalyptus globulus essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye floaters with herbal essential oils is to dilute a few drops of lavender or chamomile oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Gently apply the mixture around your eyes using a cotton swab or ring finger. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these oils can help reduce inflammation and promote healing, alleviating symptoms of eye floaters. Repeat this process 2-3 times daily for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye floaters that are treated with herbal essential oils are visual disturbances such as blurred vision, double vision, and sensitivity to light. These conditions can be alleviated by using essential oils derived from herbs like bilberry, chamomile, and lavender. Bilberry oil is particularly effective in improving circulation and reducing inflammation in the eyes, while chamomile and lavender oils promote relaxation and reduce stress-induced eye strain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with eye floaters because it improves blood circulation to the eyes, reducing the appearance of floaters. The antioxidants in ginkgo biloba neutralize free radicals that can damage the retina and cause floaters. By reducing inflammation and improving vision clarity, ginkgo biloba essential oils may help to alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with eye floaters by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of floaters.
Terpenoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce the appearance of eye floaters by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.
Bilobalide: Helps to reduce the visibility of eye floaters by improving blood flow to the eyes and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to improve vision and reduce eye floaters.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make essential oils because they contain unique compounds that may help improve blood circulation to the eyes and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.

Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are sometimes used to make essential oils, which may help to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce the occurrence of eye floaters due to their antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers through a solvent like ethanol or hexane using 250 grams of plant material per liter of solvent.
Filter the solvent-plant mixture to remove plant residue using a buchner funnel under vacuum for 30 minutes.
Distill the filtered mixture using steam distillation at 100 degrees celsius and atmospheric pressure for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the distilled solvent using a separatory funnel and 500 milliliters of dichloromethane.
Purify the essential oil using a rotary evaporator and 50 milliliters of ethanol under reduced pressure for 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger oil, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been found to reduce inflammation and improve circulation to the eyes. This can help to alleviate the condition known as eye floaters, which are caused by the accumulation of debris in the vitreous humor. remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Helps reduce inflammation in the eye, which may contribute to the visibility of eye floaters, by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Gingerol: Exhibits antioxidant properties, which may help protect the retina from oxidative stress and reduce the visibility of eye floaters.
Zingiberene: Displays anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eye, contributing to a reduction in eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the underground stems of the plant, which are often used to produce essential oils due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which may help with inflammation and pain relief.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain a high amount of essential oils and may be used to produce oils with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could potentially benefit eye health.

Leaves: The leaves of the plant are sometimes used to produce essential oils, which may contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that could help with inflammation and pain relief, potentially benefiting eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes weighing about 100 grams for extraction purposes.
Dry the rhizomes in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes in a distillation apparatus at a temperature range of 60 to 80 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus for a period of 2 to 3 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and sediment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica essential oils helps with eye floaters because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of eye floaters.
Asiatic acid: Asiatic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the retina from damage and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
Madecassoside: Madecassoside has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including triterpenoid saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Stems: Used because they contain a lower concentration of active compounds than leaves, but still contain triterpenoid saponins, making them a viable alternative for some applications.
Rhyzomes: Used because they contain a similar concentration of active compounds as leaves, but may be more readily available in some regions, making them a viable option for essential oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems on a sunny morning for optimal potency.
Steam distill the harvested material in a copper still at 100°c for 2 hours to extract oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities and ensure purity within 15 minutes.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa rhizomes when they are mature and the tops have started to yellow for drying.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm dry place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Steam distill the powder in a ratio of 1:10 with distilled water to extract the essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers at dawn when moisture levels are lowest.

Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have antioxidant properties that can protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the formation or visibility of eye floaters.

Phenolic compounds (e.g., flavonoids and phenolic acids): These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially reducing the visibility and frequency of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of eye floaters.
Roots: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing eye floaters and improving vision clarity.
Leaves: Some sources also use leaves for their antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or dried ruscus aculeatus leaves in the early morning to avoid heat stress.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area at 30°c for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a 5-liter still with a 2:1 water to herb ratio for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel and store in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any remaining impurities and contaminants.

remedies.remedy_desc: Viscum album essential oils helps with eye floaters because it promotes blood circulation and reduces inflammation in the eyes. The oil's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and exacerbate the visibility of floaters. Additionally, Viscum album's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce the size and number of floaters, allowing for clearer vision. By addressing underlying causes, Viscum album essential oils may provide relief from eye floaters and promote overall eye health. Regular use may also improve vision clarity and reduce eye strain.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: They help with eye floaters by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eye, which can contribute to the appearance of floaters.
Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): They possess antioxidant properties that help protect the retina and reduce oxidative damage, which can lead to the formation of eye floaters.

Triterpenoids (e.g., ursolic acid): They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and damage to the eye's blood vessels, potentially alleviating eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain bioactive compounds that help reduce eye floaters by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters by promoting eye health.
Stems: May be used to create essential oils that help reduce eye floaters by improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature viscum album branches in early winter when they are still green.
Steam distill 10 kg of viscum album branches for 4 hours to obtain a crude essential oil.
Filter the crude essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in airtight glass bottles in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Analyze the essential oil for its chemical composition and quality using gas chromatography.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with eye floaters because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the eyes. The calming effects of lavender oil can reduce eye strain and alleviate the appearance of floaters, which are caused by shadows cast on the retina by small particles in the vitreous gel. By promoting relaxation and reducing eye inflammation, lavender oil may help to minimize the visibility of floaters, providing relief and comfort for individuals experiencing this condition.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate eye floater symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, contributing to eye floater relief.

Caffeic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floater symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their calming and soothing properties, which may help alleviate eye strain related to eye floaters.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and calming compounds, which may help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation for eye health.
Stems: Contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate eye strain and discomfort associated with eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom when purple color is most vibrant.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a stainless steel still at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a clean glass container with a capacity of 500ml.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decantation or centrifugation within 30 minutes.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with eye floaters because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of floaters. The oil's antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the delicate tissues of the eyes, potentially reducing the visibility of floaters. Additionally, Boswellia serrata is known for its ability to promote healthy blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which can help to alleviate the underlying causes of eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eye floaters.
Furoshinol A: This sesquiterpene has shown potential in reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, which could help alleviate symptoms of eye floaters.
Alpha-pinene: As a terpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, alpha-pinene may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye floater symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make essential oils because they contain a high amount of boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve vision in individuals with eye floaters.

Barks: The barks of the Boswellia serrata tree are used to make essential oils, which contain boswellic acids that help reduce inflammation and improve eye health, potentially alleviating eye floater symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of boswellia serrata resin from healthy trees in a dry and clean environment.
Steam distill the resin in a 1:10 ratio with water at 100c for 4 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 5c for 6 months.
Test the oil for quality and purity using gc-ms analysis and spectrophotometry after 6 months.
